coaching.coaching: Plan how concrete proving command for ambient-tool prevention will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: concrete proving command for ambient-tool prevention

coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] existing AGENTS.md venv bootstrap content will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: existing AGENTS.md venv bootstrap content

coaching.coaching: Pin down file impact declaration for this leaf ticket with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: file impact declaration for this leaf ticket

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or augment the 'prevents ambient Ruff or missing mypy' AC with a concrete, observable check — e.g. specify a step that activates the ambient (non-venv) environment, runs `make lint` or `ruff check` without venv activation, shows it picks up wrong tooling, then shows the AGENTS.md-prescribed activation resolves it; or reframe as an inspectable AC ('AGENTS.md explicitly calls out the ambient-Ruff and missing-mypy failure modes by name') verified by `grep`).
evidence: No proving command is specified for this AC in the plan steps
The AC uses outcome-prevention framing ('prevents X from being mistaken') which cannot be verified by inspecting a file or running a command
The plan steps only add documentation; no step simulates the failure mode (ambient Ruff, missing mypy) and then re-runs the gates to show the guidance resolves it
findings.finding: The acceptance criterion 'The guidance prevents ambient Ruff or missing mypy from being mistaken for repository failures' is framed as a behavioral/preventive outcome with no concrete proving command. There is no described step or deterministic check that produces evidence this prevention actually works — it is entirely aspirational and unmeasurable in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'The guidance prevents ambient Ruff or missing mypy from being mistaken for repository failures'
scenarios: An agent marks this AC done purely by writing AGENTS.md, with no evidence that the documented guidance actually disambiguates the failure mode
The criterion could be satisfied by any non-empty AGENTS.md addition, making it unverifiable
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with: '- [ ] AGENTS.md explicitly names the ambient-Ruff / missing-mypy failure modes and specifies the venv activation command that avoids them (verifiable: `grep -i "ambient\|venv\|activate" AGENTS.md` returns non-empty).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before adding new AGENTS.md content, confirm what is SPECIFICALLY missing vs. already covered by AGENTS.md lines 17-23 and docs/local-dev-env.md, and scope the change to the actual gap (e.g. a Codex-specific one-liner or a missing worktree-activation reminder) rather than duplicating existing guidance.
evidence: AGENTS.md lines 17-23: 'Bootstrap the env with `make install` (not a bare `pip`/`uv pip install`)…When developing rebar itself…run the repo checkout's build, not a stale global install…see `docs/local-dev-env.md`.'
.agents/rules/rebar.md lines 16-17: 'activate the repo venv (`source .venv/bin/activate`) before any `rebar`/gate command, and confirm `which rebar` resolves to the worktree's `.venv/bin/rebar`.'

docs/local-dev-env.md lines 73-88: 'Symptom when the venv is NOT active: a hook fails with `make: mypy: No such file or directory` (or `ruff`) — even though your code is clean. It is an environment problem, not a code problem.'
AGENTS.md line 10: 'Keep this file lean: an @-import loads at launch and does not reduce context, so already-documented topics are one-line pointers into `docs/`, not restated sections.'
findings.finding: The plan proposes adding AGENTS.md guidance on venv bootstrap and activation to prevent ambient-tool confusion, but this content already exists in AGENTS.md (lines 17-23: 'Bootstrap the env with `make install`' + pointer to docs/local-dev-env.md), in .agents/rules/rebar.md (lines 16-17: 'activate the repo venv (`source .venv/bin/activate`) before any `rebar`/gate command'), and comprehensively in docs/local-dev-env.md (lines 73-88: the exact 'ambient Ruff / missing mypy' symptom and fix). Adding new AGENTS.md sections covering the same ground would duplicate content that AGENTS.md's own stated policy ('Keep this file lean') prohibits.
findings.location: Plan section 'Add concise Codex-facing AGENTS.md guidance'; AC 1 ('AGENTS.md tells Codex sessions how to bootstrap and activate'); AC 2 ('prevents ambient Ruff or missing mypy from being mistaken for repository failures')
scenarios: The plan adds a new 'activate your venv before running make lint / make typecheck' section to AGENTS.md, duplicating docs/local-dev-env.md content and violating the file's own leanness policy.
A Codex session reading AGENTS.md sees conflicting or redundant paragraphs about venv setup, increasing confusion rather than reducing it.
findings.suggested_fix: Audit AGENTS.md lines 17-23 and docs/local-dev-env.md §'The commit gate needs the dev tools on PATH'. If the gap is that Codex sessions don't follow the existing `docs/local-dev-env.md` pointer, strengthen the one-line pointer in AGENTS.md (e.g. add 'especially in Codex/isolated-worktree sessions') rather than adding a new section. If the gap is a missing worktree-specific activation step, add a single sentence to the existing Bootstrap paragraph rather than a new section.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether AGENTS.md already exists in the repository and, if so, specify in the plan how new environment-bootstrap guidance will be placed (e.g., a named section, an amendment to an existing block) to avoid duplication or conflicting instructions.
evidence: Plan text: 'Add concise Codex-facing AGENTS.md guidance that selects the repository virtual environment'
ABSENCE: no mention of whether AGENTS.md already exists or what existing content it contains
T7(c): large docs / hot-path instruction-bloat check requires structure awareness
findings.finding: The plan says 'Add concise Codex-facing AGENTS.md guidance' without stating whether an AGENTS.md file already exists and, if so, how new content will be structured or merged into it. An unacknowledged pre-existing AGENTS.md could result in duplicated, conflicting, or un-navigable guidance sections.
findings.location: Plan > 'Add concise Codex-facing AGENTS.md guidance' step; AC #1 and AC #5
scenarios: AGENTS.md already exists with Codex instructions; new section duplicates or contradicts existing guidance
A future contributor sees two conflicting environment-bootstrap blocks and follows the wrong one
findings.suggested_fix: Add a plan step: 'Check for an existing AGENTS.md; if present, add a clearly labeled ## Environment Bootstrap section rather than prepending a new file, and verify no existing guidance contradicts the new content.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'remains concise' with a measurable bound, e.g. 'adds no more than 30 lines to AGENTS.md' or 'fits in a single named section with no subsections', so the criterion is deterministically evaluable.
evidence: 'remains concise' contains no line-count limit, word-count bound, or structural constraint
Two different reviewers could reach opposite conclusions about whether a 20-line or 50-line AGENTS.md section is 'concise'
findings.finding: The acceptance criterion 'The change cites the authoritative project setup documentation and remains concise' uses 'concise' as an evaluative term with no stated threshold or measurable bound, making it a subjective criterion an executor cannot deterministically verify.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'The change cites the authoritative project setup documentation and remains concise'
scenarios: An agent writes a 100-line AGENTS.md section and considers it concise; a reviewer disagrees with no shared standard to arbitrate
findings.suggested_fix: State a concrete bound: 'The AGENTS.md addition is a single section of ≤ 25 lines.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define or remove 'rebar' in the plan body: either identify it (e.g., link to where it is documented) and add an AC verifying it, or replace it with a concrete tool name, or remove it if it is a typo.
evidence: 'running rebar, make lint, make typecheck, or other development gates' — 'rebar' appears only here, nowhere in Context, AC, or elsewhere in the plan
ABSENCE: no AC verifies rebar execution or defines what it is
findings.finding: The plan body mentions 'rebar' as a development gate alongside 'make lint' and 'make typecheck', but this term is undefined and has no corresponding acceptance criterion, leaving implementers uncertain whether it is a tool name, a typo, or an alias for something else.
findings.location: Plan step 2: 'running rebar, make lint, make typecheck, or other development gates'
scenarios: Implementer adds AGENTS.md guidance referencing 'rebar' without knowing what it is, producing misleading documentation
Implementer omits 'rebar' from AGENTS.md because it is undefined, but it was actually an important gate
findings.suggested_fix: If 'rebar' is a typo for a Make target (e.g., 'make check' or similar), correct it. If it is a real tool, add a brief description and an AC such as 'AGENTS.md mentions rebar and its role alongside make lint / make typecheck'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale sentence to the Plan section explaining why AGENTS.md is the correct layer (e.g., 'because `.codex/config.toml` designates AGENTS.md as the `project_doc_fallback_filenames` source that Codex loads at session start, making it the single-source touch point for all harnesses including Codex').
evidence: Plan text: 'Add concise Codex-facing AGENTS.md guidance that selects the repository virtual environment before running rebar, make lint, make typecheck, or other development gates.' — no sentence explains WHY AGENTS.md is the right layer.
`.codex/config.toml` line 7: `project_doc_fallback_filenames = ["AGENTS.md"]` — this is the mechanism that makes AGENTS.md the correct target, but the plan doesn't cite it.
The G6 criterion requires the plan to state a POSITIVE rationale for the chosen approach; its absence is a finding.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Plan' section does not state a positive rationale for why AGENTS.md is the chosen layer for Codex environment guidance (rather than, for example, a `.codex/config.toml` setup command or shell profile). The rationale is implicit to a reader familiar with the codebase but absent from the plan text itself.
findings.location: Plan section — 'Add concise Codex-facing AGENTS.md guidance…'
scenarios: A reviewer or future author cannot verify the approach is sound without independently discovering `.codex/config.toml` — the rationale should be self-contained in the plan.
An implementer might question whether to put the guidance in `.codex/config.toml` directly instead, leading to a wrong implementation.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Plan section, prepend a sentence such as: 'Codex reads `AGENTS.md` natively at session start (via `.codex/config.toml` `project_doc_fallback_filenames`) — making it the correct and minimally-invasive layer for persistent environment guidance that also serves Claude Code and other harnesses without duplication.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
